significant_events.source_quote: The financial year saw the united strengths of corporates, institutional orgnaisations coming forward to help animals affected by Covid-19. Our paramount focus was to relieve animals of hunger. Thousands of these animals were affected due to closure of regular eateries, restaurants, stalls and other establishments during the government imposed lockdown. HCL Foundation, Infoysys Foundation, Azim Premji Foundation, Ashraya Hastha Trust, Robert Bosch Engineering & Business Solutions Pvt Ltd, Walmart Global Tech India, Tally Solutions Pvt Ltd and many more generous organisations came forward to provide uninterrupted supply of feed to stray and shelter animals. A total of 5000+ animals were fed on a daily basis.
significant_events.summary: Provided daily feed to over 5000 stray and shelter animals during the COVID-19 lockdown with corporate support.

significant_events.source_quote: Since the program's inception, CUPA has successfully neutered over 3000 cats with the support from Help Animals India, the HCL Foundation, Robert Bosch Engineering & Business Solutions Pvt Ltd and cat lovers from across the city. The TNVR project involves a team of veterinary doctors, attenders, and supervisors on the ground working with residents of gated communities to trap, neuter as many cats as possible over the course of the programme.
significant_events.summary: Implemented a Trap-Neuter-Release (TNR) program for community cats, neutering over 3000 cats with support from partners.

significant_events.event_name: Inauguration of CUPA Small Animal Specialty Hospital
significant_events.source_quote: The latest upgrade to our bouquet of services the CUPA Small Animal Specialty Hospital, RT Nagar was initiated in June 2020 through CSR initiatives. The Centre was inaugurated in January 2021, with members of the public thrilled at seeing a state of the art facility providing reasonable health care services for pets.
significant_events.summary: Inaugurated a new 5000 sq ft state-of-the-art veterinary hospital in RT Nagar, providing subsidized healthcare for pets.

significant_events.source_quote: One such breeder case that took the city by storm was the Puttenahalli Station breeder case, where upon a complaint being filed, we found nearly a dozen breed dogs housed in the worst conditions, really exposing the darkness of the breeding industry. Lack of veterinary care, food, shelter and even ventilation, left these dogs in the worst of health conditions, becoming a health hazard to humans as well. We were successful in taking custody of these dogs through a landmark decision of the Karnataka High Court.
significant_events.summary: Successfully intervened in the Puttenahalli Station breeder case, taking custody of neglected breed dogs through a Karnataka High Court decision.

significant_events.source_quote: From two tiny rooms in her house, CUPA today operates from seven centres in the city of Bangalore.
significant_events.summary: Established the first and largest community-driven sterilization program for animals in Bangalore.

significant_events.source_quote: Empowers to rescue, provide relief, and prevent cruelty to animals through medical care, adoption, and sterilization.
significant_events.summary: Involved in legal issues protecting wild and domestic animal welfare in the High Court of Karnataka and Supreme Court.

significant_events.source_quote: CUPA has also recently initiated the T.N.R. program for cats.
significant_events.summary: Engages in pet cruelty inspections and advocates for animal rights.

significant_events.source_quote: It also helps in the rehabilitation and housing of wildlife in their indigenous forest zones.
significant_events.summary: Recently initiated a Trap-Neuter-Release (TNR) program for cats.

significant_events.source_quote: CUPA is also involved with legal issues protecting the interest and welfare of animals, wild and domestic, in the High Court of Karnataka and the Supreme Court.
significant_events.summary: Provides rehabilitation and housing for wildlife in indigenous forest zones.

data.original_text: CUPA mainly takes care of animals that are hurt, sick or abandoned and gives them care and treatment until they recover, after which they are re-homed or rehabilitated.

The Karnataka-based organisation has a network of 7 centres, each dedicated to a critical aspect of animal well-being, Animal Birth Control, Trauma & Rescue Centre, Second Chance Adoption Centre, Geriatric Centre, and Large Animal Rehabilitation Rescue Centre, accommodating around 300 animals in total.
CUPA also engages in ventures such as pet cruelty inspections and advocacy for animal rights.
CUPA has also recently initiated the T.N.R. program for cats.
It also helps in the rehabilitation and housing of wildlife in their indigenous forest zones.
CUPA is also involved with legal issues protecting the interest and welfare of animals, wild and domestic, in the High Court of Karnataka and the Supreme Court.
Empowers to rescue, provide relief, and prevent cruelty to animals through medical care, adoption, and sterilization.
One such initiative involves sponsoring meals at the CUPA Large Animal Rescue & Rehabilitation Centre, providing nourishment for large working animals like cows, bulls, and goats.
Another opportunity is to sponsor meals at the CUPA Geriatric Centre, which houses over 60+ blind and handicapped senior dogs in need of ongoing care.
Additionally, contributions to the trauma and rescue centre can support free medical, surgical, and post-operative care for injured, sick, or abandoned animals.

The CUPA Small Animal Specialty Hospital is a modern facility offering comprehensive veterinary care for pets. With a skilled team of veterinarians, it provides a range of services from cosmetic procedures to medical surgeries. The hospital also offers housing and post-operative care facilities. Its services include vet consultations, grooming, sterilization, vaccinations, diagnostic imaging (X-ray, ultrasound, ECG), blood tests, surgeries, trauma care, and a pet shop with a pharmacy.
This well-established animal welfare organisation runs seven centres in Bangalore, including a veterinary hospital and a geriatric centre.

They have a Corporate Social Responsibility programme under which you can help with donations, sponsorships or passive adoption of their shelter animals, employee volunteering activities or group visits at their shelters, along with hosting awareness programmes, fundraisers or adoption drives for employees or on corporate campuses.
CUPA is actively involved with the rescue and rehabilitation of ill, injured and abandoned street animals in Bangalore.
CUPA is a 29-year-old organisation that has undertaken a number of diverse avatars in order to rescue and rehabilitate injured and sick animals across Bangalore City.
The organisation has carried out tireless work towards animal welfare over the decades.
This is the first and largest community-driven sterilization program in Bangalore.
From two tiny rooms in her house, CUPA today operates from seven centres in the city of Bangalore.
More than 10,000 wild and domestic animals pass through its portals
Over the years, it has treated a significant number of animals, totalling 398,121.

Compassion Unlimited Plus Action has impacted, 1,200,122 animals positively affected, including 4,861 adoptions, 166,689 dogs sterilized, 344,681 dogs vaccinated, 3,041 cats sterilized, 3,042 cruelty cases attended, 149,606 emergency trauma cases, 86,489 large animals treated.
The CUPA Trauma & Rescue Centre... has treated a significant number of animals, with a total of 149,606 cases attended to.
Additionally, the centre has facilitated 329 adoptions, finding loving homes for animals in need.
